success_surface: Successfully implemented group-based conditional logic for marking maximum values across related data segments, handling ties, nulls, and edge cases while preserving workbook structure.
effective_behaviors: Exploratory data inspection before implementing the solution
Sequential grouping logic using delimiter detection patterns
Intermediate result logging for logic validation
Edge case handling for ties and single-item groups
Consistent cell addressing using numeric indices
evidence: Agent first ran exploratory code to inspect sheet structure, max dimensions, and sample data before building final solution
Groups were identified by scanning a delimiter column and accumulating entries until empty cell appeared
Multiple print statements verified group counts, member data, and final output values
Tie handling was explicit: all members with max points received the same marker value
Used ws.cell(row=X, column=Y) consistently instead of mixing notation styles
Final verification loop confirmed values written to target column Z

memory_items.title: Data Structure Discovery Before Manipulation
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect the workbook structure and sample data before implementing transformation logic
memory_items.generalization: Use programmatic inspection (sheet names, max dimensions, sample rows) to understand data layout before executing modifications

memory_items.title: Delimiter-Based Group Detection
memory_items.lesson: When processing grouped data blocks, identify group boundaries using delimiter patterns (empty cells or specific markers)
memory_items.generalization: Accumulate entries into temporary collections until a delimiter condition is met, then process each collection independently

memory_items.title: Incremental Validation Through Logging
memory_items.lesson: Print intermediate results at key processing stages to verify logic correctness before final output
memory_items.generalization: Log group counts, sample data, and partial outputs during execution to catch logic errors early

memory_items.title: Edge Case Coverage for Aggregate Operations
memory_items.lesson: Handle ties, single-item groups, and null values explicitly when computing maximum/aggregate values per segment
memory_items.generalization: When finding extrema within groups, ensure all ties receive the same outcome and empty/single groups have defined behavior

memory_items.title: Consistent Cell Addressing Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Use numeric column indices consistently throughout the script rather than mixing string notation
memory_items.generalization: Choose one addressing method (A1 or numeric) and apply it uniformly across all read/write operations

memory_items.title: Post-Writing Output Verification
memory_items.lesson: After writing results, sample-read from the target cells to confirm values were applied correctly
memory_items.generalization: Validate a subset of written cells immediately after save operations to detect write failures

edits.content:
## 6. Advanced Group Processing
- **Pre-Manipulation Discovery**: Always run diagnostic queries to understand sheet dimensions and data patterns before implementing transformations.
- **Delimiter Detection**: For block-style data, scan for empty/null delimiters to segment groups; accumulate entries until delimiter found, then process independently.
- **Tiered Validation**: Log intermediate results (group counts, sample values, partial outputs) at multiple stages to catch logic errors early.
- **Aggregate Edge Cases**: When computing max/min within groups, explicitly handle ties (all tied values get same treatment) and single-member groups.
- **Numeric Addressing Consistency**: Use column numbers or A1 notation uniformly across all operations—do not mix within a single script.
- **Write Verification Loop**: After saving, sample-read from target cells to confirm values were written successfully before reporting completion.
